title: Squad Bundle Frontmatter Schema
description: Validates the structural content of a squad bundle's three canonical artifacts (team-personas.md, pitfalls.yaml, task-chains.yaml) as merged into a single validation envelope. This schema is consumed by the Phase 2 bundle-validator script (not yet authored). It does NOT validate file existence — that is the responsibility of the bundle-completeness check in validate-governance.sh. ADR-009 §bundle-completeness governs the 5-artifact requirement; this schema enforces per-artifact internal structure.

team_personas_h1_present.description: True when team-personas.md contains an H1 heading matching '# Team Personas — <Domain> Squad'. The validator MUST confirm the heading text starts with '# Team Personas'.

team_personas_squad_vetoes_present.description: True when team-personas.md contains the '## Squad vetoes' H2 section followed by a markdown table with columns 'Persona' and 'VETO scope'.

team_personas_veto_count.description: Number of VETO personas declared in the Squad vetoes table. ADR-009 requires at minimum 2 VETO holders; 3 is the typical pattern from the 4 exemplars.

team_personas_persona_count.description: Total number of named persona H3 sections in team-personas.md. ADR-009 requires at least 3; the 4 exemplars all have 5.

pitfalls.description: Array of pitfall entries parsed from pitfalls.yaml top-level 'pitfalls:' key. ADR-009 requires ≥10; the 4 exemplars have 12-18.

task_chains.description: Array of task chain entries parsed from task-chains.yaml top-level 'task_chains:' key. ADR-009 requires ≥2.

pitfall_entry.description: A single pitfall entry. Mirrors the format of .claude/pitfalls-catalog.yaml and all 4 ADR-009-compliant exemplar pitfalls.yaml files.

id.description: Pitfall id in the form PREFIX-NNN (uppercase prefix, 3-digit zero-padded monotonic number). Examples: EDTECH-001, DEVREL-003, SALES-012.

rule.description: Imperative rule statement. Should begin with 'NEVER' or an affirmative obligation verb. Minimum 20 characters to prevent placeholder stubs.

whenToUse.description: One sentence describing the trigger context for this pitfall. Minimum 10 characters to prevent placeholder stubs.

agents.description: List of persona names from the domain's team-personas.md that are responsible for enforcing this rule.

items.description: Persona name as it appears in team-personas.md (e.g. 'Priya Narayanan').

task_chain_entry.description: A single task chain entry. Mirrors the format in the 4 ADR-009-compliant exemplar task-chains.yaml files.

id.description: Task chain id in the form <domain-slug>-<chain-slug> (all lowercase kebab-case). Example: edtech-onboard-new-school-district.

title.description: Human-readable chain title. Should be imperative or descriptive of the workflow.

whenToUse.description: 2-5 sentences describing the trigger condition and scope boundaries for this chain.

steps.description: Ordered steps in the chain. ADR-009 exemplars have 7-9 steps; minimum is 4 to prevent trivially thin chains.

verification.description: Optional multi-line verification block listing acceptance criteria for this chain. Present in all 4 exemplars.

task_chain_step.description: A single step within a task chain.

id.description: Step identifier. Typically an integer (1, 2, 3...) but may be a string for sub-steps.

owner.description: Persona name and role responsible for this step. E.g. 'Priya Narayanan (Student Privacy Engineer)'.

action.description: Imperative action sentence describing what the owner does in this step.

skill.description: Optional skill reference if a named skill governs this step.
